stories.title: Utah Talons Win 7th Straight — 10 Games Left in AUSL Regular Season
stories.angle: The Talons' 7-game streak is the story — they are turning a strong season into a dominant final push. The doubleheader tonight gives fans two must-watch games in the standings race.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
The Utah Talons have won 7 straight.

They're 14-5 and in first place with 10 games left in the AUSL regular season.

The No. 1 seed earns a bye straight to the Championship Series.

The Talons are running away with this thing. #AUSL #Softball

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
AUSL doubleheader tonight:

🥎 7:00 PM ET — Carolina Blaze vs. Texas Volts (Dell Diamond, Round Rock TX)
🥎 9:30 PM ET — Chicago Bandits vs. Portland Cascade (Hillsboro Ballpark)

Utah's already 14-5. Everyone else needs these wins.

The race is on. #AUSL #Softball

article.headline: Utah Talons Roll to Seven-Game Win Streak With 10 Games Left in AUSL Season

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Utah Talons Win 7th Straight

**Image Concept 1B — Social (1080x1350)**

research.facts:
Utah Talons 7-Game Win Streak
**Source(s):**
- AUSL official team page (theausl.com/talons/news) — headline: "Talons roll to the top of the standings on seven-game win streak" (newly indexed as of July 10 morning)
- AUSL schedule confirmation: Talons vs. Spark played July 9 at Tom Heath Field, Oklahoma City, 8 PM ET on ESPNU
- AUSL schedule for July 10: Blaze vs. Volts (7 PM ET, Dell Diamond, Round Rock TX); Bandits vs. Cascade (9:30 PM ET, Hillsboro Ballpark, Hillsboro OR)
**Confirmed Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Talons won the Spark game July 9 (headline explicitly confirms win and 7-game streak)
- Talons likely at 14-5 after win (was 13-5 heading into July 9 game)
- Regular season ends July 20; 10 games remaining
- No. 1 seed earns direct bye to Championship Series (July 25-27, Davis Diamond, College Station TX); play-in is July 23
- Tonight's doubleheader: Blaze vs. Volts 7 PM ET at Dell Diamond; Bandits vs. Cascade 9:30 PM ET at Hillsboro Ballpark
- Bandits were 10-7 heading into July 10 games (did not play July 9)
- Blaze and Cascade were 8-7 heading into July 10 games
**Confirmed Facts (MEDIUM confidence):**
- Exact score of Talons-Spark game not found in secondary sources; win is confirmed by headline, score not confirmed
- Patty Gasso was in ESPNU broadcast booth for the Talons-Spark game (KSL.com, OurSports Central confirmation of scheduled broadcast debut)
**Notes:** Exact score not available from sources accessed. Using team record inference (13-5 + 1 win = 14-5) and headline confirmation.

stories.title: MLBx All-Star 3-On-3 Is SUNDAY — NFL Stars Confirmed Alongside AUSL Players
stories.angle: The NFL additions are the new hook today — this is no longer just softball vs. MLB legends. It's a multi-sport spectacle. Two days out, this is the most exciting event of the week for softball fans.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
MLBx: All-Star 3-on-3 is THIS SUNDAY.

AUSL: Brito. Jennings. Garcia. Canady.
MLB: Howard. Rollins. Victorino. Andruw Jones.
NFL: DeVonta Smith. Terrell Owens.

Co-ed. Multi-sport. Citizens Bank Park. Peacock.

July 12. Don't miss it. #Softball #AllStarWeek

article.headline: MLBx All-Star 3-on-3 Is Sunday — NFL Stars Join AUSL Players for Philadelphia Showcase

research.facts:
MLBx All-Star 3-on-3 — Sunday July 12
**Source(s):**
- MLB.com/all-star/sunday/mlbx
- AUSL news release (theausl.com/news/mlbx-all-star-3-on-3-canady-brito-garcia-jennings/)
- MLB.com press release (June 23 announcement)
- Front Office Sports, Line Drive Media, KSNT (Canady local profile)
**Confirmed Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Event: Sunday July 12 at Citizens Bank Park, following the All-Star Futures Game, on Peacock
- AUSL participants: Alyssa Brito (Team Howard/Ryan Howard), Tiare Jennings (Team Rollins/Jimmy Rollins), Rachel Garcia (Team Victorino/Shane Victorino), NiJaree Canady (Team Jones/Andruw Jones)
- Format: Two semi-finals + one final, 30 minutes each; co-ed, mixing MLB legends, NFL stars, and AUSL players
- NFL participants confirmed: DeVonta Smith, Terrell Owens among the participants (co-ed competition)
- Brito: 3x WCWS champion at Oklahoma, first-time U.S. WNT member
- Jennings: 4x WCWS champion at Oklahoma
- Garcia: Olympic silver medalist (Tokyo 2020), 2019 WCWS champion (UCLA)
- Canady: 2026 AUSL 2nd overall pick, 2x NFCA Pitcher of the Year
**Confirmed Facts (MEDIUM confidence):**
- The original format details (2-minute at-bat, hot streak bonus, center-field target) from July 9 remain accurate per MLB.com event page

stories.title: Patty Gasso Made Her Broadcast Debut Last Night — Alameda Is Next
stories.angle: From announcement to actual debut. Gasso did it live last night — a milestone for professional softball's broadcast identity.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Patty Gasso made her broadcast debut last night on ESPNU.

Eight national titles. Now calling AUSL games.

She's back in the booth Saturday. Lonni Alameda joins Saturday too.

The best coaches in softball history are calling the best players. #AUSL #Softball

article.headline: Patty Gasso Made Her Broadcast Debut Last Night. The AUSL Is Better For It.

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Patty Gasso Broadcast Debut

**Image Concept 3A — Social (1080x1350)**
- **Type:** Debut announcement card
- **Concept:** "BROADCAST DEBUT" header. Patty Gasso action or headshot image. Below: "Patty Gasso · AUSL booth · ESPNU." 8 championship trophy icons or reference. "Back Saturday on MLB Network." I Love Softball logo. Professional, milestone feel.
- **Imagn search terms:** "Patty Gasso Oklahoma"
- **Mode:** Canva composition
- **Status:** not_started

research.facts:
Patty Gasso Broadcast Debut
**Source(s):**
- KSL.com: "Patty Gasso to make broadcast debut for Athletes Unlimited Softball League"
- OurSports Central: Gasso+Alameda announcement (oursportscentral.com/services/releases/patty-gasso-lonni-alameda-to-join-ausl-booth-this-week)
- Fox Sports: Gasso broadcast debut announcement
**Confirmed Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Gasso (8 national titles, Oklahoma) debuted in ESPNU booth July 9 for Talons-Spark game
- Gasso is scheduled for Saturday July 12 (8 PM ET, MLB Network, Talons at Oklahoma City — second game)
- Alameda (Louisiana-Lafayette) scheduled for Saturday July 11 (8 PM ET, MLB.TV)
- Gasso previously served as USA national team head coach (in addition to Oklahoma HC role)
- 8 national titles = most in college softball history

stories.title: Megan Grant ESPYS — 5 Days to Vote
stories.angle: Tight five-day window — final voting push before the July 15 ceremony.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
5 days left to vote for Megan Grant at the ESPYs.

42 home runs. A 31-year record. She's up against the NFL, the Olympics, and the marathon world.

One vote per day at ESPN.com.

The window is closing. Use your votes. #Softball #ESPYs

article.headline: 5 Days Left to Vote for Megan Grant's ESPY — Here's What the Award Means

image_concepts.label: Image

---

research.facts:
Megan Grant ESPYS
**Source(s):**
- AUSL/Cascade team page (theausl.com/cascade/news/vote-megan-grant-2026-espy/)
- SI.com: "NCAA Softball highlighted in 2026 ESPYs Awards nominations"
- UCLA Athletics: Vote page
- ESPN Press Room: ESPYS announcement (34th annual ceremony)
**Confirmed Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Category: Best Record-Breaking Performance
- Grant's feat: 42 HR in 2026 at UCLA, breaking Laura Espinoza's 1995 Arizona record of 37 HR
- Grant is now playing for Portland Cascade (No. 4 overall pick, 2026 AUSL College Draft)
- Also set UCLA career HR record: 91 HR, passing Stacey Nuveman
- Competitors: Myles Garrett (NFL/LA Rams), Johannes Høsflot Klæbo (cross-country skiing, multiple Olympic gold medals), Sabastian Sawe (Kenya, long-distance running)
- Voting: Once per day at ESPN.com, through July 15
- 34th annual ESPYs air July 15, 8 PM ET on ABC and ESPN app (presented by Capital One)
- Host: Marcello Hernández

stories.title: PGF Nationals 9 Days — Roster Freeze in 3 Days
stories.angle: The automatic-release rule means rosters are in flux RIGHT NOW — coaches and families must act before the July 13 freeze locks everything in.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
3 days until the PGF Nationals roster freeze.

July 13 is the deadline. July 19 is first pitch at Great Park, Irvine.

And the clock is already running — automatic releases have been active since July 1.

Is your roster locked? #PGFNationals #Softball

article.headline: PGF Nationals Are 9 Days Away — And Rosters Are Changing Right Now

research.facts:
PGF Nationals
**Source(s):**
- PGF Nationals Week One (pgfnationals.com/week-one)
- PGF National Qualifiers
- PGF Rulebook (automatic release rule as of July 1)
**Confirmed Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Event: July 19, Great Park Irvine (8000 Great Park Blvd, Irvine CA 92618)
- Week One: 18U and 16U
- Roster freeze: July 13 at 11:59 PM PST (3 days from today)
- Transfer portal open through July 31
- September 1 contact window opens in 53 days
- Automatic release rule: As of July 1, players from non-qualified/non-invited teams released automatically — late roster moves active now
